summary: Matched two small miscellaneous functions across the audio library and name-entry menu: lbAudioAx_80024DC4 in lbaudio_ax.c and mnName_8023749C in mnname.c. The audio match required recognizing lbl_80433710 as a larger pool-allocation state object containing an HSD_ObjAllocData header plus channel/id arrays, then updating existing allocator calls to use the embedded alloc field. The menu match added a typed signature for mnName_8023749C and implemented language-dependent name-table lookup with an opt_common_subs pragma to preserve matching.
what_changed: Replaced the nonmatching stub for lbAudioAx_80024DC4 with a function that scans a 16-entry audio pool for an existing arg0 value, refreshes its x70 priority/timer value to 0xA if found, otherwise claims the first sentinel slot whose x2C entry equals 0x83D60 and initializes that slot.
Changed lbl_80433710 from a plain HSD_ObjAllocData static to a typed lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc struct with an embedded HSD_ObjAllocData alloc field at 0x00, x2C[17], and x70[17], size noted as 0xB4.
Updated HSD_ObjFree, HSD_ObjAlloc, and HSD_ObjAllocInit call sites in lbaudio_ax.c to pass &lbl_80433710.alloc instead of &lbl_80433710 after the static object was retyped.

Replaced the nonmatching stub for mnName_8023749C with a char* returning implementation that selects mnNameNew_803EE724 for saved US language and mnNameNew_803EE720 otherwise, scans name entries up to the requested slot or a terminator marker, and returns NULL if the resolved entry begins with mnName_StringTerminator.
Added lb/lblanguage.h include and extern declarations for mnNameNew_803EE720 and mnNameNew_803EE724 in mnname.c.
Updated mnname.h to give mnName_8023749C the concrete prototype char* mnName_8023749C(int slot) instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
smart_moves: Retyping lbl_80433710 as a wrapper struct exposed hidden state after the object allocator header, making lbAudioAx_80024DC4 expressible as normal C while preserving allocator usage through the embedded alloc field.
Kept allocator API calls semantically correct after the static retyping by passing the embedded HSD_ObjAllocData field rather than relying on pointer aliasing to the whole wrapper object.

Used explicit signed and unsigned casts around mnName_StringTerminator, slot, and array byte comparisons, which likely helped reproduce the original branch/register behavior for the matched menu function.
Applied #pragma opt_common_subs off only around mnName_8023749C, a targeted matching tactic that avoids globally changing optimization behavior.
Promoted a previously unknown header declaration to a real return type and parameter list once the implementation established the function contract.
decomp_lessons: If a static symbol is used both as an HSD_ObjAllocData and as storage for additional arrays, model it as a struct with HSD_ObjAllocData at offset 0 and update allocator calls to use the embedded member.
For pool-management functions, look for a two-pass pattern: first refresh an existing active entry, then locate a sentinel/free entry and claim it.
Sentinel values embedded in arrays, such as 0x83D60 in lbAudioAx_80024DC4, can be part of the matching logic and should not be prematurely abstracted without evidence.
Menu/name-list functions may need language-dependent global string-table selection via lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS before indexing or scanning entries.
When matching byte-oriented string terminator logic, signedness matters; preserving s8/u8 casts can be necessary for exact codegen.
Narrow pragmas such as opt_common_subs off are acceptable matching aids when confined to the specific function that needs them.
naming_conventions: New local struct name follows subsystem prefixing: lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc.
Unknown fields in newly discovered structs retained offset-style names such as x2C and x70 pending stronger semantic evidence.
Embedded allocator field was named alloc, clarifying the relationship between the wrapper struct and HSD_ObjAllocData.
Existing decomp placeholder function names were preserved for unmatched-address symbols: lbAudioAx_80024DC4 and mnName_8023749C.
The concrete mnName_8023749C prototype uses descriptive parameter name slot, matching its apparent role in selecting a name-list entry.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced a typed static wrapper struct to align field offsets: HSD_ObjAllocData at 0x00, x2C at 0x2C, and x70 at 0x70.
Used two separate pointers, p and q, both initialized to st->x2C in lbAudioAx_80024DC4, likely to influence register allocation or aliasing for the match.
Used fixed loop bound 0x10 for the audio pool scan even though the arrays are declared length 17.
Compared free audio slots with an explicit unsigned cast: (u32) q[i] == 0x83D60U.
Wrapped mnName_8023749C in #pragma push / #pragma opt_common_subs off / #pragma pop.
Used an empty-body for loop in mnName_8023749C to encode the scan compactly while preserving the original control flow shape.
Preserved explicit casts to s8 and u8 in the name-table scan and terminator check.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR summary reports fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for all functions.

The human text excerpt describes the name-entry function as appending a string terminator byte, but the diff evidence for mnName_8023749C shows language-dependent name lookup and NULL-on-terminator behavior; treat the append description as weak or possibly inaccurate for this function.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc fields x2C and x70 with semantic audio-channel names.
Check whether other lbaudio_ax functions access lbl_80433710 beyond the HSD_ObjAllocData header and can benefit from the same wrapper struct.
Search for later cleanup around mnName_8023749C to confirm whether its behavior is name lookup, terminator append, or both in surrounding call context.
Find other mnname functions that use mnNameNew_803EE720, mnNameNew_803EE724, or mnName_StringTerminator for consistent typing.
